register.note: Forced 0 and earned it: hero.png is a text-only title+lede+meta card with no specimen, whereas ciechanowski-watch/00-viewport.png lands a poster-worthy rendered watch and invites manipulation right under the fold.

Forced 0: there is no hero specimen at all, so unlike seeing-theory-regression/01 which shows the OLS line plus the squared-residual squares as the mechanism, nothing above the spine depicts the boundary or probability field.

The plot carries axis labels, a TRAINED caption, and an in-figure accuracy/log-loss/class-hue strip, but the boundary line and probability gradient stay unlabeled (P=0.5, sure-vs-guessing live only in prose) — distill-momentum/02 instead writes its labels onto the artifact itself with per-mode captions tied to the inequality.

Soft continuous gold-to-blue field, clean ringed points, calm focal hierarchy on warm ground — Distill-soft and competent, but a plain d3 scatter on a rectangle with a utilitarian verdict strip, well short of the hand-drawn coil and dual-slider craft of ciechanowski-watch/02.

A Train button plus a visible step slider (step 0) scrubs the descent re-tracing the boundary and a probability-field toggle is explanatory scaffolding, but from static frames I cannot confirm it reads as cleanly as the staged cost/classification reveals in 3b1b-gradient-descent/02, so it holds at competent.

Lab grammar is held throughout — amber=class0/truth, blue=class1/prediction, red reserved for misclassified points and 'wrong side'/log-loss, teal=active control — and the big field is a two-hue class gradient (no error wash); like r2d3-trees/00 the encoding stays consistent, and ours extends it into AA-tinted prose terms that R2D3 does not.

hero.note: A hero would need the trained boundary plot rendered full-width above the spine — gold/blue field, dashed line, a few ringed misclassified points, one in-graphic label — composed as a portrait legible as a thumbnail.

assessment.note: Item 04 is a hands-on lab task (work the Raw to Add-x1-squared toggle, completed with your hands) and item 05 is an open applied transfer about equipment-failure prediction at both temperature extremes, so the stack is not pure MCQ.

blocking: No hero specimen above the spine — add the trained decision-boundary plot as a full-width composed portrait so the two forced-0 DELIGHT dimensions can clear their floor of 3.
Annotation lives beside the figure, not on it — label the boundary as the decision boundary and mark P=0.5 / sure vs guessing in-canvas to lift annotation-integration off 2.
Atmosphere is clean but plain — add depth/finish to the scatter (softer field edges, a more crafted verdict strip) to move past competent toward the watch bar.
verdict.summary: Disciplined colour and a solid in-canvas plot, but with no hero the two DELIGHT floors fail and annotation/atmosphere/motion sit at competent — holds short of register 3.
